news.title: IBAN mağdurlarına yasal düzenleme. 12. Yargı Paketi'ne eklenmesi bekleniyor

news.title: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
news.summary: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

news.title: UN says it will evacuate sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz, as Rubio warns against tolls
news.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned Iran that no country can charge fees for ships to travel through the strait.

news.title: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say
news.summary: Israel's military says the two men were Hezbollah operatives, but the Iran-backed group accuses it of a ceasefire violation.

news.title: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction
news.summary: Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours.

news.title: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
news.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

news.title: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children
news.summary: Israel rejects the new report by the three-member expert panel, calling it a "libellous sham".

news.title: Syrians reject Trump’s call for Syria to combat Hezbollah in Lebanon
news.summary: Residents in Damascus rejected US President Donald Trump's suggestion that Syria should confront Hezbollah in Lebanon.

news.title: Budimir gives Croatia a 1-0 win to eliminate Panama from the World Cup
news.summary: Croatia are third in Group L, with England and Ghana on four points each, heading into the final round of matches.

news.title: US Senate votes to pause Iran war in rare rebuke to Trump
news.summary: US Senate votes to pause Iran war in rare rebuke to Trump

news.title: England’s Spence appears to refuse handshake with booed Ghana player Partey
news.summary: Social media footage shows Djed Spence appearing to decline a handshake with Partey during pre-match introductions.

news.title: Iran war live: Trump, Tehran at odds over nuclear inspections, Hormuz
news.summary: US and Iran offer conflicting statements as negotiators push towards reaching a final, comprehensive deal.

news.title: UN begins evacuation of 11,000 sailors stranded in Strait of Hormuz
news.summary: The UN's maritime agency launched an operation to evacuate more than 11,000 sailors stranded in the Strait of Hormuz.

news.title: Mamdani's political gamble pays off as his endorsed candidates sweep their primaries
news.summary: All three progressive candidates backed by New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani — including two who took on Democratic incumbents — won their primaries in safe seats almost guaranteeing their election in November.

news.title: Supreme Court rules that prison guards can't be sued for shaving Rastafarian's head
news.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.

news.title: In symbolic vote, Congress directs Trump to remove forces from Iran war
news.summary: The measure to remove U.S. armed forces from hostilities with Iran does not require the president's signature, nor does it carry the force of law. But it reflects bipartisan frustration with the war.

news.title: Congress passes the largest housing affordability bill in decades
news.summary: An expansive bipartisan bill intended to bring down the cost of housing by boosting the supply of homes has passed both houses of Congress, and is headed to the president's desk for a signature.

news.title: Is AI 'one big bubble'? Behind the tech sell-off
news.summary: Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."

news.title: Portugal's Ronaldo shakes off World Cup doubters, scores 2 after sluggish start
news.summary: At 41, Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the oldest players in the World Cup. Against the Democratic Republic of Congo he was mostly invisible and questions mounted. Against Uzbekistan, he sparkled.

facts.text: Honey never spoils — edible honey has been found in ancient tombs.
A day on Venus is longer than its year.
Bananas are berries; strawberries are not.
